stories.title: Transfer Portal Opens Tomorrow — The Hottest Names to Watch

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: The softball transfer portal opens TOMORROW — 15 days, June 8-22. FSU's Jaysoni Beachum (.417 BA, 16 HRs, 66 RBI) headlines the list of names to watch. The offseason chess match starts at midnight. 🥎 #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: LSU's Tori Edwards — the 2025 SEC Freshman of the Year with a .802 slugging percentage — is expected in the portal when it opens tomorrow. Two years of eligibility. Top 25 programs will be calling. #CollegeSoftball #SoftballPortal

research.facts:
Transfer Portal Opens Tomorrow — Hot Names to Watch
**Source: SI.com / Yahoo Sports / Extra Inning Softball / On3**
- Transfer portal for undergraduates opens June 8, 15-day window through June 22
- Graduate students and players with coaching changes could already enter
- Tennessee's Saviya Morgan (sophomore outfielder) and Kailey Plumlee (freshman pitcher) already announced intent to enter (covered June 6 as setup story)
- **Jaysoni Beachum (FSU)** — All-American third baseman, final year of eligibility: .417 BA, 78 hits, 16 HRs, 66 RBI, .509 OBP in 2026; FSU had 4 NFCA All-Americans; announced intent to move on
- **Tori Edwards (LSU)** — redshirt sophomore, 2025 All-American and SEC Freshman of Year, set LSU single-season record with .802 slugging percentage; 2 years of eligibility remaining
- **LSU pitcher** — 2.81 ERA, 128 Ks, 13-9 record in 139.1 IP; entering portal with 2 years remaining
- On3 is tracking the full 2026-27 softball transfer portal at on3.com
- Extra Inning Softball launched "Switchboard" to elevate athlete visibility during portal window
- Every major program expected to be active buyers in the portal
**Confidence: HIGH** (multiple sources confirming portal timeline and named players)

stories.title: 2026 College Softball Season Awards — The Year's Best Recognized

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: The 2026 college softball awards are in. Megan Grant (UCLA) and Jordy Frahm (Nebraska) named USA TODAY top All-Americans. Patrick Murphy (Alabama) is Coach of the Year. A historic season getting its proper recognition. 🥎 #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: NFCA All-Americans: Jordy Frahm and Kaitlyn Terry on the First Team. Teagan Kavan, Katie Stewart, NiJaree Canady, and Mia Williams on the Second Team. Texas Tech had four All-Americans despite losing the championship. #WCWS #CollegeSoftball

research.facts:
2026 College Softball Season Awards
**Source: Yahoo Sports / USA TODAY / Texas Tech / NFCA.org / SI.com**
**USA TODAY Sports Network All-Americans (top honors):**
- Megan Grant (UCLA) — senior, set NCAA single-season HR record (40+, entered WCWS at 40, surpassing Arizona's Laura Espinoza 37 from 1995), .469 BA, 87 RBI
- Jordy Frahm (Nebraska) — two-way star: 20-4 pitcher, 12 saves, 1.14 ERA, 234 Ks; .416 BA, 19 HRs, 50 RBI as hitter
- Patrick Murphy (Alabama) — Coach of Year after leading Crimson Tide to No. 1 overall seed
**NFCA Division I All-Americans:**
- First Team: Jordy Frahm (Nebraska), Kaitlyn Terry (Texas Tech), among others
- Second Team: Teagan Kavan (Texas), Katie Stewart (Texas), NiJaree Canady (Texas Tech), Mia Williams (Texas Tech)
- Third Team: Jackie Lis (Texas Tech)
- Texas Tech had 4 NFCA All-Americans: Terry (1st), Canady (2nd), Williams (2nd), Lis (3rd)
- FSU had 4 NFCA All-Americans: Isa Torres (1st), Ashtyn Danley (2nd), Jazzy Francik (3rd), Jaysoni Beachum (3rd)
- Texas's four NFCA honorees announced May 27
**Confidence: HIGH** (texaslonghorns.com, texastech.com, NFCA.org, Yahoo Sports)

research.facts:
NiJaree Canady Turns Pro — AUSL No. 2 Pick
**Source: Texas Tech Athletics / AUSL / MLB.com / ESPN / KFYO**
- Canady selected No. 2 overall in 2026 AUSL College Draft by the Texas Volts
- First golden ticket recipient (presented by commissioner Kim Ng and Patrick Mahomes on March 27)
- Career stats: 1,000+ strikeouts, 96 wins — only active pitcher with those marks
- 3x All-American (last three seasons)
- Top 10 ERA in each of the last four regular seasons
- First collegiate player to sign NIL deal with AUSL including profit participation units
- Drafted from Topeka, Kansas
- AUSL = Athletes Unlimited Softball League
**Confidence: HIGH** (official AUSL.com, texastech.com, MLB.com)

research.facts:
Post-WCWS Coaching Carousel
**Source: SI.com / MSN / NFCA Coaching Transactions**
- **Illinois**: fired Tyra Perry after 11 years — 2026 record: 15-39 overall, 3-20 Big Ten
- **Minnesota**: relieved Piper Ritter — 2026 record: 16-36
- **Towson**: Lisa Costello retires after 33 seasons — career record 857-773-3; retirement effective July 8
- **Morehead State**: Megan Griffith resigned, effective June 10
- **Youngstown State**: Brian Campbell retired after 18 seasons — 435 career wins, 3 Horizon League regular-season titles; 30 years coaching total
- **Longwood**: Megan Brown not returning after 6 seasons, 153-170 record, 7-10 conference in 2026
- Coaching carousel article at SI.com tracking all moves
**Confidence: HIGH** (SI.com coaching carousel tracker, NFCA)

First look at 2027 college softball:

No. 4 Nebraska (WCWS momentum)

🥎 #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: 2027 Early Power Rankings: Texas Is No. 1, But Who Has the Best Shot to Dethrone the Longhorns?

- **Imagn search terms:** "Teagan Kavan Texas" | "Texas softball"

research.facts:
2027 Early Power Rankings
**Source: Synthesis from prior research + story context**
- **Texas**: Clear No. 1 — Kavan and Stewart both returning juniors; Mike White coaching staff intact; defending back-to-back champs; one of only 5 programs to win consecutive WCWS titles
- **Alabama**: Jocelyn Briski returning (junior); Patrick Murphy coaching; SEC powerhouse
- **Texas Tech**: Loses Canady but returns Williams (2nd NFCA), Terry (1st NFCA), Lis; strong program base under Gerry Glasco
- **Nebraska**: Post-Frahm era; program on the rise after first WCWS in 13 years; Coach Rhonda Revelle building
- **Tennessee**: Significant portal losses (Morgan, Plumlee + Pickens graduated); Karen Weekly must rebuild
- **Oklahoma**: First WCWS miss since 2015; Patty Gasso still coaching; expect bounce-back
- **UCLA**: Loses Grant (graduated) and others; rebuilding
**Confidence: HIGH** for Texas/Alabama/TexasTech (confirmed returns); MEDIUM for others (program trajectory)
